mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when GDP growth falls below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the moving average crossover fails to predict direction with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, higher borrowing costs constrain corporate leverage and cap equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a regime of falling inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal yields negative forward 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal renewed construction activity and upstream supply chain demand that supports near-term equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of rising housing starts periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index above 3% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity discount factors and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict direction when real rates remain negative.
